piq library installed (optional, for metrics validation)

5011de0f-5ff8-46f0-9ce3-337ea337c9de.notes:
Phase 1: Foundation - Required before any implementation work

cfbca66f-00e7-437b-b8b5-a4ce37d16261.title: Download and organize test datasets
cfbca66f-00e7-437b-b8b5-a4ce37d16261.description: Download Berkeley 500 dataset, CSIQ image database, and CMU PIE face database for testing and evaluation.

Dataset README created with source URLs

cfbca66f-00e7-437b-b8b5-a4ce37d16261.notes:

Verified sources: BSDS500 from Kaggle (kaggle.com/datasets/balraj98/berkeley-segmentation-dataset-500-bsds500), CSIQ from Qualinet (qualinet.github.io/databases/image/categorical_image_quality_csiq_database/). CSIQ has contrast decrement category specifically.

PARTIAL COMPLETION: 10 sample images from scikit-image generated (camera, coins, moon, page, astronaut, chelsea, plus synthetic low_contrast, high_contrast, gradient, checkerboard). External datasets (Berkeley 500, CSIQ) require Kaggle/University registration - placeholder directories created with download instructions. Sample images sufficient for development and testing.

BLOCKED: External datasets (Berkeley 500, CSIQ) require manual registration on Kaggle/University sites. Sample images (10 from scikit-image) are sufficient for development. Task can be completed when user downloads external datasets manually.

74f40c0f-72d4-4441-a4f0-4c226b92e083.title: Implement spatial histogram computation
74f40c0f-72d4-4441-a4f0-4c226b92e083.description: Implement 2D spatial histogram computation for each gray level. For each gray level x_k, count its occurrences in M×N spatial grids. Grid size computed dynamically using K (distinct gray levels) and image aspect ratio.

success_criteria.text: Function compute_spatial_histogram(X, x_k, M, N) returns correct h_k matrix
Grid size calculation matches formula (2): N=floor(sqrt(K/r)), M=floor(sqrt(K*r))
Aspect ratio preserved in grid division
Unit test passes for 3x3 test image

74f40c0f-72d4-4441-a4f0-4c226b92e083.notes:
Phase 2: SECE Core - Implements formula (1) and (2) from paper. Key innovation: uses spatial distribution instead of gray-level histogram.

Reference implementation: github.com/xiezhongzhao/Contrast_Enhancement (26 stars). Key: Grid size M×N uses aspect ratio r = H/W.

2c467730-74a9-4ead-8da9-8812522d9b38.title: Implement spatial entropy calculation
2c467730-74a9-4ead-8da9-8812522d9b38.description: Calculate spatial entropy S_k for each gray level using the 2D spatial histogram. Uses Shannon entropy formula: S_k = -Σ_m Σ_n h_k(m,n) * log2(h_k(m,n)). The entropy measures how spread out each gray level is in the image space.

success_criteria.text: Function compute_spatial_entropy(h_k) returns correct entropy value
Entropy formula matches equation (3) from paper
Handles edge case: entropy = 0 when h_k is empty or uniform
Unit test: single-pixel grid returns entropy = 0

2c467730-74a9-4ead-8da9-8812522d9b38.notes: Phase 2: SECE Core - Implements formula (3). Higher entropy = gray level more spread across image.

61e7e8dc-e247-4d2e-99fd-188aa1a83d31.title: Implement distribution function calculation
61e7e8dc-e247-4d2e-99fd-188aa1a83d31.description: Calculate discrete function f_k and cumulative distribution function F_k from spatial entropies. Formula (4): f_k = S_k / sum(S_l where l != k). Formula (5): normalize f_k. Formula (6): F_k = cumulative sum of f_l.

success_criteria.text: f_k formula matches equation (4): relative importance measure
CDF F_k matches equation (6) and is monotonically increasing
Unit tests pass for edge cases

61e7e8dc-e247-4d2e-99fd-188aa1a83d31.notes: Phase 2: SECE Core - Implements formulas (4), (5), (6). The relative importance f_k measures how significant each gray level is compared to others.

b72dc5d6-431e-491d-b05b-b12f9e54c193.title: Implement gray level mapping function
b72dc5d6-431e-491d-b05b-b12f9e54c193.description: Map input gray levels to output gray levels using the CDF. Formula (7): y_k = floor(F_k * (y_u - y_d) + y_d). Default output range is [0, 255] for 8-bit images.

success_criteria.text: Function mapping_function(F, y_d=0, y_u=255) returns output gray levels
Returns uint8 array for image compatibility

b72dc5d6-431e-491d-b05b-b12f9e54c193.notes: Phase 2: SECE Core - Implements formula (7). Maps CDF to output dynamic range.

c4466777-e646-478b-bf34-481c0997bc48.title: Implement complete SECE function
c4466777-e646-478b-bf34-481c0997bc48.description: Integrate all components into complete SECE (Spatial Entropy-based Contrast Enhancement) algorithm. Input: grayscale image X. Output: enhanced image Y. Also returns distribution function f for use in SECEDCT.

success_criteria.text: Function SECE(X) returns enhanced image Y and distribution f
Output image uses full dynamic range [0, 255]
Preserves shape of input histogram (key feature)
No visual artifacts on test images

c4466777-e646-478b-bf34-481c0997bc48.notes:
Phase 2: SECE Core - Integrates all SECE components. Key property: preserves histogram shape while enhancing contrast.

Phase 2: SECE Core - Integrates all SECE components. Key property: preserves histogram SHAPE while enhancing contrast. IMPORTANT: Returns both enhanced image Y and distribution f (needed by SECEDCT for alpha calculation). Default output range [0,255].

example_output.png deferred until Image I/O module is implemented. Enhancement verified: original range 50-149 expanded to 2-254.

ddac6cfd-78c2-43db-bd25-c5a8121cea35.title: Implement 2D-DCT transform functions
ddac6cfd-78c2-43db-bd25-c5a8121cea35.description: Implement forward and inverse 2D Discrete Cosine Transform using SciPy fftpack. Forward transform: dct2d(X) returns coefficients D. Inverse transform: idct2d(D) returns image. Implements formulas (8), (9), (13) from paper.

success_criteria.text: All unit tests in test_dct.py pass

ddac6cfd-78c2-43db-bd25-c5a8121cea35.notes:
Phase 3: SECEDCT - Implements formulas (8), (9), (13). Uses scipy.fftpack.dct with norm=ortho.

Use scipy.fftpack.dct with norm='ortho' for orthonormal DCT. OpenCV also has cv2.dct() but scipy is more flexible.

be93ff54-bda1-42b0-8b86-3eaa61d87911.title: Implement DCT coefficient weighting
be93ff54-bda1-42b0-8b86-3eaa61d87911.description: Implement weighting function for DCT coefficients using formula (11): w(k,l) = (1 + (alpha-1)*k/(H-1)) * (1 + (alpha-1)*l/(W-1)). Also implement alpha calculation formula (12): alpha = entropy^gamma.
be93ff54-bda1-42b0-8b86-3eaa61d87911.notes:
Phase 3: SECEDCT - Implements formulas (10), (11), (12)

Phase 3: SECEDCT - Implements formulas (10), (11), (12). IMPORTANT: w(0,0)=1 guarantees no change to DC coefficient (mean value preserved). Alpha uses entropy of distribution f: α = (Σf_k·log₂f_k)^γ

d54006f3-681c-438a-85f0-fdba614f8dcd.title: Implement complete SECEDCT function
d54006f3-681c-438a-85f0-fdba614f8dcd.description: Integrate SECE, DCT transform, and coefficient weighting into complete SECEDCT algorithm. Flow: SECE(X) -> Y_global -> dct2d(Y_global) -> weight_coefficients -> idct2d -> Y_final. Gamma parameter controls local contrast enhancement level.

success_criteria.text: SECEDCT(X, gamma=0) output equals SECE(X) output

d54006f3-681c-438a-85f0-fdba614f8dcd.dependencies: SECEDCT calls SECE internally for global enhancement before DCT-based local enhancement
d54006f3-681c-438a-85f0-fdba614f8dcd.notes:
Phase 3: SECEDCT - gamma=0 means no local enhancement (equals SECE), gamma=1 means maximum local enhancement

Key parameter: gamma in [0,1]. gamma=0 equals SECE output (no local enhancement), gamma=1 gives maximum local enhancement. Start testing with gamma=0.5.

Phase 3: SECEDCT - gamma=0 means no local enhancement (equals SECE), gamma=1 means maximum local enhancement. ALGORITHM FLOW: 1) Call SECE(X) to get Y_global and distribution f, 2) DCT(Y_global) to get D, 3) Compute alpha = entropy(f)^gamma, 4) Weight D coefficients using alpha, 5) IDCT to get Y_final. Default gamma=0.5 per paper.

Phase 3: SECEDCT - gamma=0 means no local enhancement (equals SECE), gamma=1 means maximum local enhancement. ALGORITHM: 1) SECE(X) returns Y_global AND distribution f, 2) DCT(Y_global) -> D, 3) alpha = entropy(f)^gamma, 4) weight D using alpha, 5) IDCT -> Y_final. Default gamma=0.5 per paper.

43b212eb-fb08-4db2-8254-ea4672738665.title: Implement EMEG contrast metric
43b212eb-fb08-4db2-8254-ea4672738665.description: Implement Expected Measure of Enhancement by Gradient (EMEG) from formula (14). Divides image into blocks, computes gradient-based contrast measure for each block, averages results. Higher EMEG indicates higher contrast.

success_criteria.text: SSIM range is [-1, 1], typically [0, 1] for images

43b212eb-fb08-4db2-8254-ea4672738665.dependencies: Metrics need SECE implementation to measure enhancement
43b212eb-fb08-4db2-8254-ea4672738665.notes:
Phase 4: Evaluation - EMEG measures contrast enhancement level. Block size default 8x8.

Consider piq library (pip install piq) for validation - production IQA with 38 metrics including gradient-based measures.

Phase 4: Evaluation - EMEG measures contrast enhancement level. Block size default 8x8. WARNING: EMEG is highly sensitive to noise - high EMEG may indicate noise rather than good contrast. For valid enhancement: EMEG(Y) > EMEG(X) expected.

SSIM added: uses skimage.metrics.structural_similarity with multichannel=True for color images. Threshold 0.9 for perceptual similarity. Part of Phase 3: Metrics & Baselines.

CLARITY: This task implements both EMEG (primary) and SSIM (secondary) metrics. SSIM was merged here because: 1) Both are evaluation metrics used together, 2) SSIM implementation is simple (skimage wrapper), 3) Reduces task count. Success criteria includes both metrics. Deliverables include both emeg.py and ssim.py files.

pytest tests/test_emeg.py passes with 38 tests

Coverage > 80% for metrics module

All EMEG and SSIM success criteria verified in tests

All deliverables complete: emeg.py, test_emeg.py, ssim.py

6afc7775-aeb2-4593-a7da-e471e625755f.title: Implement GMSD distortion metric
6afc7775-aeb2-4593-a7da-e471e625755f.description: Implement Gradient Magnitude Similarity Deviation (GMSD) metric for measuring distortion between original and enhanced images. Uses Sobel gradients to compute gradient magnitude similarity map, then takes standard deviation as final score. Lower GMSD indicates less distortion.
6afc7775-aeb2-4593-a7da-e471e625755f.dependencies: GMSD needs SECE to compare original vs enhanced
6afc7775-aeb2-4593-a7da-e471e625755f.notes:
Phase 4: Evaluation - GMSD measures perceptual distortion. Reference: W. Xue et al., IEEE TIP 2014.

Production implementation available in piq library (torchmetrics also has GMSD). Constant c=0.0026 per original paper. piq.gmsd() returns (0, inf), lower is better.

Phase 4: Evaluation - GMSD measures perceptual distortion. Reference: W. Xue et al., IEEE TIP 2014. Threshold: GMSD > 0.1 indicates visually noticeable/distorting distortion. Ideal: GMSD(X,Y) ≈ 0. piq library has production implementation.

Starting implementation: Creating gmsd.py and test_gmsd.py

8c6ca77b-46b0-46ab-b274-a95e88d6024c.title: Implement color image enhancement
8c6ca77b-46b0-46ab-b274-a95e88d6024c.description: Extend SECE and SECEDCT to color images using HSV color space. Only process V (value/luminance) channel while preserving H (hue) and S (saturation) channels. Convert BGR -> HSV -> process V -> convert back to BGR.

success_criteria.text: color_sece preserves H and S channels
color_secedct preserves H and S channels
color_sece supports HSV, LAB, YCbCr color spaces
ColorProcessor abstract class with to_luminance/from_luminance methods

8c6ca77b-46b0-46ab-b274-a95e88d6024c.dependencies: Requires SECE and SECEDCT grayscale implementations
8c6ca77b-46b0-46ab-b274-a95e88d6024c.notes:
Phase 4: Color Support - HSV space preserves color perception while enhancing luminance contrast.

Extended: HSV (default, perceptually uniform), LAB (device-independent), YCbCr (video standard). Use Strategy Pattern for color processors. OpenCV codes: COLOR_BGR2HSV, COLOR_BGR2LAB, COLOR_BGR2YCrCb.

Design decision: Used separate color_sece()/color_secedct() functions instead of adding color_space parameter to existing SECEEnhancer class. This keeps grayscale and color APIs cleanly separated and avoids breaking changes. ColorProcessor ABC with strategy pattern supports HSV, LAB, YCbCr color spaces. All tests pass (53 tests, 98% coverage).

Blocker #d54006f3-681c-438a-85f0-fdba614f8dcd is completed. Task should now be unblocked.

DESIGN DECISION: Color support implemented via separate color_sece()/color_secedct() functions in sece/color/__init__.py instead of adding color_space parameter to SECEEnhancer class. This provides cleaner API separation between grayscale and color processing, avoids breaking changes to existing API, and follows single-responsibility principle. The color module effectively serves as 'color.py' with full color processing support (HSV, LAB, YCbCr).

Deliverable fulfilled by sece/color/__init__.py which provides comprehensive color processing including ColorProcessor ABC, HSVProcessor, LABProcessor, YCbCrProcessor, color_sece(), color_secedct(), and supporting functions.

7cf67922-b7f6-4fae-9a2e-e3ab16080702.description: Run SECE and SECEDCT on Berkeley 500 dataset. Measure EMEG and GMSD for all images. Generate comparison statistics against baseline algorithms (GHE, EHS, WTHE). Create CSV report with quantitative results.

7cf67922-b7f6-4fae-9a2e-e3ab16080702.notes: Phase 5: Testing - Quantitative validation on standard benchmark dataset.

60acd33e-8f3a-460a-8bdf-be20ef38fafd.title: Implement baseline comparison algorithms
60acd33e-8f3a-460a-8bdf-be20ef38fafd.description: Implement GHE (Global Histogram Equalization) and WTHE (Weighted Thresholded Histogram Equalization) as baseline algorithms for comparison. These are standard methods referenced in the paper.

success_criteria.text: GHE output uses full dynamic range [0, 255]

60acd33e-8f3a-460a-8bdf-be20ef38fafd.notes:
Phase 5: Baselines - Reference implementations for quantitative comparison.

WTHE PyPI package: imWeightedThresholdedheq (github.com/Mamdasn/imWeightedThresholdedheq). GHE uses cv2.equalizeHist(). CLAHE uses cv2.createCLAHE().

361ab3d7-d57c-48cb-83e8-1062f7c87aae.description: Write comprehensive documentation including README.md with installation instructions, API documentation for all public functions, usage examples with code snippets, and comparison results visualization.

success_criteria.text: README.md contains API reference for SECE and SECEDCT

361ab3d7-d57c-48cb-83e8-1062f7c87aae.notes: Phase 5: Documentation - Final deliverables for sharing the implementation.

8ae76b01-8df5-4fd1-9534-bb5a10d0e3b1.title: Implement image I/O utilities with format detection
8ae76b01-8df5-4fd1-9534-bb5a10d0e3b1.description: Create io module with automatic image format detection, loading, and saving. Handle grayscale, color (RGB/BGR), and RGBA images with alpha channel stripping. Support PNG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP, WEBP formats. Include 16-bit to 8-bit conversion with scaling.

success_criteria.text: save_image() supports format selection via path or parameter
RGBA images converted to RGB with UserWarning

8ae76b01-8df5-4fd1-9534-bb5a10d0e3b1.dependencies: Needs Python environment setup for OpenCV
8ae76b01-8df5-4fd1-9534-bb5a10d0e3b1.notes: Use cv2.imread with cv2.IMREAD_UNCHANGED for format preservation. Add ensure_uint8() helper for bit depth conversion. Part of Phase 2: Color & I/O

0eb65914-05bb-463b-a970-ef279bd57e52.title: Implement PyTorch GPU backend for DCT transforms
0eb65914-05bb-463b-a970-ef279bd57e52.description: Create TorchBackend class implementing the Backend abstract interface. Uses torch-dct library or custom PyTorch implementation for 2D-DCT/IDCT transforms on GPU. Auto-detects CUDA availability and falls back to CPU. Memory-efficient for 4GB VRAM constraint.

success_criteria.text: TorchBackend.dct2d() produces same results as NumpyBackend within 1e-6
Auto-detects CUDA and uses GPU when available
Falls back gracefully when CUDA unavailable
512x512 image processed in < 0.2s on RTX 3050 Ti

deliverables.text: tests/test_backends.py comparing NumPy vs Torch outputs

0eb65914-05bb-463b-a970-ef279bd57e52.dependencies: GPU backend needs DCT weighting implementation as reference
0eb65914-05bb-463b-a970-ef279bd57e52.notes:
Install: pip install torch-dct. Use torch.cuda.is_available() for detection. Check VRAM with torch.cuda.get_device_properties(). Part of Phase 4: GPU & Advanced. Optional dependency in pyproject.toml.

STARTED: Creating backends module with Backend ABC, NumpyBackend, and TorchBackend

IMPLEMENTED: Backend module with abstract Backend ABC, NumpyBackend (scipy.fftpack), and TorchBackend (torch-dct). Auto-detects CUDA with graceful CPU fallback. All tests pass (319 passed). Performance test for 512x512 < 0.2s requires GPU - CPU fallback test allows < 2s. pyproject.toml updated with torch-dct dependency in [gpu] extras.

1f4ddb01-ea3d-45c7-8b71-f9a3450bd764.title: Implement CLI tool with Click and Rich
1f4ddb01-ea3d-45c7-8b71-f9a3450bd764.description: Create command-line interface using Click for argument parsing and Rich for progress bars and formatted output. Support single image and batch folder processing, format detection, method selection (sece/secedct), gamma parameter, metrics calculation, baseline comparison, and device selection (cpu/cuda).

success_criteria.text: sece input.png -o output.png processes single image
Rich progress bar shows processing status

1f4ddb01-ea3d-45c7-8b71-f9a3450bd764.dependencies: Requires image I/O, SECE, and SECEDCT implementations
1f4ddb01-ea3d-45c7-8b71-f9a3450bd764.notes: Entry point in pyproject.toml: [project.scripts] sece='sece.cli.main:cli'. Use @click.group() for subcommands, Rich Progress() for batch processing. Part of Phase 2: Color & I/O
